name: pods

metadata.name: pods
metadata.summary: Python software for Open Data Science

documentation:
pods ===

# Python open data science software. This repository contains utilities and tools for open data science including tools for accessing data sets in python. # Installation ``` pip install pods ``` ## Release Notes Note that from version 0.0.32-alpha non-data set loading functionality has been moved to other packages. Check [`goggles`](https://github.com/lawrennd/goggles), [`assessor`](https://github.com/lawrennd/assessor) and [`notutils`](https://github.com/lawrennd/notutils).

functions.name: pods.access.kepler_telescope_urls_files
documentation:
Find which resources are missing on the local disk for the requested Kepler datasets.

```
param star_datasets

the star data sets to be checked for.

type star_datasets

tuple of lists containg kepler ids and data sets.
```

functions.name: pods.access.cmu_urls_files
documentation:
Find which resources are missing on the local disk for the requested CMU motion capture motions.

```
param subj_motions

the subject motions to be checked for.

type subj_motions

tuple of lists containing subject numbers and motion numbers.
```

functions.name: pods.mocap.rotation_matrix
documentation:
Compute the rotation matrix for an angle in each direction. This
is a helper function for computing the rotation matrix for a given
set of angles in a given order.

```
param xangle

rotation for x-axis.

param yangle

rotation for y-axis.

param zangle

rotation for z-axis.

param order

the order for the rotations.
```

functions.name: pods.mocap.load_text_data
documentation: Load in a data set of marker points from the Ohio State University C3D motion capture files (http://accad.osu.edu/research/mocap/mocap_data.htm).
Utilities for loading in x,y,z data.

functions.name: pods.mocap.parse_text
documentation: Parse data from Ohio State University text mocap files (http://accad.osu.edu/research/mocap/mocap_data.htm).

functions.name: pods.mocap.read_connections
documentation: Read a file detailing which markers should be connected to which for motion capture data.

methods.name: pods.mocap.tree.find_children
documentation:
Take a tree and set the children according to the parents.

Takes a tree structure which lists the parents of each vertex
and computes the children for each vertex and places them in.

methods.name: pods.mocap.tree.find_parents
documentation:
Take a tree and set the parents according to the children

Takes a tree structure which lists the children of each vertex
and computes the parents for each vertex and places them in.

methods.name: pods.mocap.tree.find_root
documentation: Finds the index of the root node of the tree.

methods.name: pods.mocap.tree.get_index_by_id
documentation: Give the index associated with a given vertex id.

methods.name: pods.mocap.tree.get_index_by_name
documentation:
Give the index associated with a given vertex name.

```
param name

the name of a vertex.

type name

string

rval index

index of the vertex.

rtype int

```

methods.name: pods.mocap.tree.order_vertices
documentation: Order vertices in the graph such that parents always have a lower index than children.

methods.name: pods.mocap.tree.swap_vertices
documentation:
Swap two vertices in the tree structure array.
swap_vertex swaps the location of two vertices in a tree structure array.

```
param tree

the tree for which two vertices are to be swapped.

param i

the index of the first vertex to be swapped.

param j

the index of the second vertex to be swapped.

rval tree

the tree structure with the two vertex locations swapped.
```

documentation: Data from Erich Friedman's website about packing squares in squares.

functions.name: pods.datasets.football_data
documentation: Football data from English games since 1993. This downloads data from football-data.co.uk for the given season.

functions.name: pods.datasets.spellman_yeast
documentation: This is the classic Spellman et al 1998 Yeast Cell Cycle gene expression data that is widely used as a benchmark.

functions.name: pods.datasets.spellman_yeast_cdc15
documentation: These are the gene expression levels from the CDC-15 experiment of Spellman et al (1998).

functions.name: pods.datasets.lee_yeast_ChIP
documentation: Yeast ChIP data from Lee et al.

functions.name: pods.datasets.fruitfly_tomancak
documentation: Fruitfly gene expression data from Tomancak et al.

functions.name: pods.datasets.google_trends
documentation:
Data downloaded from Google trends for given query terms. Warning,
if you use this function multiple times in a row you get blocked
due to terms of service violations.

The function will cache the result of any query in an attempt to
avoid this. If you wish to refresh an old query set refresh_data
to True. The original function is inspired by this notebook:

http://nbviewer.ipython.org/github/sahuguet/notebooks/blob/master/GoogleTrends%20meet%20Notebook.ipynb

But the update makes use of `pytrends`

functions.name: pods.datasets.oil
documentation: The three phase oil data from Bishop and James (1993).

functions.name: pods.datasets.pumadyn
documentation: Data from a simulation of the Puma robotic arm generated by Zoubin Ghahramani.

functions.name: pods.datasets.silhouette
documentation: Ankur Agarwal and Bill Trigg's silhoutte data.

functions.name: pods.datasets.decampos_digits

functions.name: pods.datasets.ripley_synth
documentation: Synthetic classification data set generated by Brian Ripley for his Neural Networks book.

functions.name: pods.datasets.mauna_loa
documentation: CO2 concentrations from the Mauna Loa observatory.

functions.name: pods.datasets.osu_run1
documentation: Ohio State University's Run1 motion capture data set.

functions.name: pods.datasets.toy_rbf_1d
documentation:
Samples values of a function from an RBF covariance with very small noise for inputs uniformly distributed between -1 and 1.

```
param seed

seed to use for random sampling.

type seed

int

param num_samples

number of samples to sample in the function (default 500).

type num_samples

int
```

functions.name: pods.datasets.toy_linear_1d_classification
documentation: Simple classification data in one dimension for illustrating models.

functions.name: pods.datasets.airline_delay
documentation: Airline delay data used in Gaussian Processes for Big Data by Hensman, Fusi and Lawrence

functions.name: pods.datasets.hapmap3
documentation:
The HapMap phase three SNP dataset - 1184 samples out of 11 populations.

SNP_matrix (A) encoding [see Paschou et all. 2007 (PCA-Correlated SNPs...)]:
Let (B1,B2) be the alphabetically sorted bases, which occur in the j-th SNP, then

      /  1, iff SNPij==(B1,B1)
Aij = |  0, iff SNPij==(B1,B2)
      \ -1, iff SNPij==(B2,B2)

The SNP data and the meta information (such as iid, sex and phenotype) are
stored in the dataframe datadf, index is the Individual ID,
with following columns for metainfo:

    * family_id   -> Family ID
    * paternal_id -> Paternal ID
    * maternal_id -> Maternal ID
    * sex         -> Sex (1=male; 2=female; other=unknown)
    * phenotype   -> Phenotype (-9, or 0 for unknown)
    * population  -> Population string (e.g. 'ASW' - 'YRI')
    * rest are SNP rs (ids)

More information is given in infodf:

    * Chromosome:
        - autosomal chromosemes                -> 1-22
        - X    X chromosome                    -> 23
        - Y    Y chromosome                    -> 24
        - XY   Pseudo-autosomal region of X    -> 25
        - MT   Mitochondrial                   -> 26
    * Relative Positon (to Chromosome) [base pairs]

functions.name: pods.datasets.cifar10_patches
documentation: The Candian Institute for Advanced Research 10 image data set. Code for loading in this data is taken from this Boris Babenko's blog post, original code available here: http://bbabenko.tumblr.com/post/86756017649/learning-low-level-vision-feautres-in-10-lines-of-code

functions.name: pods.datasets.movie_collaborative_filter
documentation: Data set of movie ratings as generated live in class by students.

functions.name: pods.datasets.olympic_sprints
documentation: All olympics sprint winning times for multiple output prediction.

functions.name: pods.datasets.movie_body_count
documentation: Data set of movies and body count for movies scraped from www.MovieBodyCounts.com created by Simon Garnier and Randy Olson for exploring differences between Python and R.

functions.name: pods.datasets.movie_body_count_r_classify
documentation: Data set of movies and body count for movies scraped from www.MovieBodyCounts.com created by Simon Garnier and Randy Olson for exploring differences between Python and R.

functions.name: pods.datasets.movielens100k
documentation: Data set of movie ratings collected by the University of Minnesota and 'cleaned up' for use.

functions.name: pods.datasets.nigeria_nmis_facility_database
documentation: A rigorous, geo-referenced baseline facility inventory across Nigeria is created spanning from 2009 to 2011 with an additional survey effort to increase coverage in 2014, to build Nigeria’s first nation-wide inventory of health facility. The database includes 34,139 health facilities info in Nigeria.

functions.name: pods.datasets.crescent_data
documentation:
Data set formed from a mixture of four Gaussians. In each class two of the Gaussians are elongated at right angles to each other and offset to form an approximation to the crescent data that is popular in semi-supervised learning as a toy problem.

> ```
> param num_data_part
>
> number of data to be sampled (default is 200).
>
> type num_data
>
> int
>
> param seed
>
> random seed to be used for data generation.
>
> type seed
>
> int
> ```

functions.name: pods.datasets.creep_data
documentation: Brun and Yoshida's metal creep rupture data.

functions.name: pods.datasets.ceres
documentation: Twenty two observations of the Dwarf planet Ceres as observed by Giueseppe Piazzi and published in the September edition of Monatlicher Correspondenz in 1801. These were the measurements used by Gauss to fit a model of the planets orbit through which the planet was recovered three months later.

functions.name: pods.datasets.kepler_lightcurves
documentation: Load Kepler light curves from David W. Hogg & Kate Storey-Fisher's NeurIPS 2020 Tutorial as shown in this colab https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1TimsiQhhcK6qX_lD951H-WJDHd92my61?usp=sharing

functions.name: pods.datasets.kepler_telescope
documentation: Load a given kepler_id's datasets.

functions.name: pods.datasets.cmu_mocap_49_balance
documentation: Load CMU subject 49's one legged balancing motion that was used by Alvarez, Luengo and Lawrence at AISTATS 2009.

functions.name: pods.datasets.cmu_mocap_35_walk_jog
documentation: Load CMU subject 35's walking and jogging motions, the same data that was used by Taylor, Roweis and Hinton at NIPS 2007. but without their preprocessing. Also used by Lawrence at AISTATS 2007.

functions.name: pods.datasets.cmu_mocap_high_five
documentation: Load the CMU Motion capture for the high 5 between subjects 20 and 21 in the motion capture data. The data was used by Lawrence and Moore ICML 2007. Later the work was recreated by Damianou and Lawrence at AISTATS 2013.

functions.name: pods.datasets.cmu_mocap
documentation: Load a given subject's training and test motions from the CMU motion capture data.

functions.name: pods.datasets.hapmap3
documentation:
The HapMap phase three SNP dataset - 1184 samples out of 11 populations.

SNP_matrix (A) encoding [see Paschou et all. 2007 (PCA-Correlated SNPs...)]:
Let (B1,B2) be the alphabetically sorted bases, which occur in the j-th SNP, then

      /  1, iff SNPij==(B1,B1)
Aij = |  0, iff SNPij==(B1,B2)
      \ -1, iff SNPij==(B2,B2)

The SNP data and the meta information (such as iid, sex and phenotype) are
stored in the dataframe datadf, index is the Individual ID,
with following columns for metainfo:

    * family_id   -> Family ID
    * paternal_id -> Paternal ID
    * maternal_id -> Maternal ID
    * sex         -> Sex (1=male; 2=female; other=unknown)
    * phenotype   -> Phenotype (-9, or 0 for unknown)
    * population  -> Population string (e.g. 'ASW' - 'YRI')
    * rest are SNP rs (ids)

More information is given in infodf:

    * Chromosome:
        - autosomal chromosemes                -> 1-22
        - X    X chromosome                    -> 23
        - Y    Y chromosome                    -> 24
        - XY   Pseudo-autosomal region of X    -> 25
        - MT   Mitochondrial                   -> 26
    * Relative Positon (to Chromosome) [base pairs]
